en.extract:
Stella's lorikeet (Charmosyna stellae) is a species of parrot in the family Psittaculidae. It is endemic to New Guinea, where its natural habitat consists of subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy
Stella's lorikeet was formerly considered conspecific with the Papuan lorikeet, but phylogenetic evidence indicates that the two are distinct species, and it has been recognized as a separate species by the IUCN Red List, BirdLife International, and the International Ornithologists' Union.
Stella's lorikeet has three recognized subspecies:

C. s. stellae - southeastern New Guinea
C. s. goliathina - western and central New Guinea
C. s. wahnesi - endemic to the Huon Peninsula

'''Stella's lorikeet''' ('''''Charmosyna stellae''''') is a species of [[parrot]] in the family [[Psittaculidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[New Guinea]], where its natural [[habitat]] consists of subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

==Taxonomy==
Stella's lorikeet was formerly considered conspecific with the [[Papuan lorikeet]], but phylogenetic evidence indicates that the two are distinct species, and it has been recognized as a separate species by the [[IUCN Red List]], [[BirdLife International]], and the [[International Ornithologists' Union]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-05-29|language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite report|title=IOC World Bird List 11.2|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|language=en|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.11.2|doi-access=free}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Stella's Lorikeet (Charmosyna stellae) - BirdLife species factsheet|url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/stellas-lorikeet-charmosyna-stellae/details|access-date=2022-01-11|website=datazone.birdlife.org}}</ref>

Stella's lorikeet has three recognized [[subspecies]]:<ref>{{cite web |last1=McCullough |first1=Carl |title=Stella's Lorkeet, What A bird! |url=https://journals.tdl.org/watchbird/index.php/watchbird/article/view/921 |website=AFA Watchbird, Journal of the American Federation of Aviculture |access-date=20 September 2018}}</ref>

* ''C. s. stellae'' - southeastern [[New Guinea]]
* ''C. s. goliathina'' - western and central New Guinea
* ''C. s. wahnesi'' - endemic to the [[Huon Peninsula]]
